papers.title: AIRA_2: Overcoming Bottlenecks in AI Research Agents
papers.innovation: Identifies and provides solutions for three structural performance bottlenecks in AI research agents: synchronous single-GPU execution (constraining sample throughput), a generalization gap where validation-based selection degrades performance over extended searches, and the lack of generalizable evaluation metrics.
papers.benchmarks: Aims to improve sample throughput, enhance generalization performance over prolonged search periods, and provide more robust validation-based selection for agent development.
papers.use_case: Designing and implementing more efficient, scalable, and robust AI research agents, particularly beneficial for engineers developing complex autonomous systems or optimizing large-scale agent training infrastructure.

papers.title: GUIDE: Resolving Domain Bias in GUI Agents through Real-Time Web Video Retrieval and Plug-and-Play Annotation
papers.innovation: Introduces a novel approach to mitigate domain bias in GUI agents by leveraging real-time web video retrieval and a plug-and-play annotation mechanism. This allows agents to gain familiarity with domain-specific software operations without extensive pre-training on proprietary data.
papers.benchmarks: Addresses significant domain bias in GUI agents, leading to improved performance and reliability when interacting with unfamiliar or specialized software interfaces.
papers.use_case: Developing highly adaptable and generalizable GUI automation agents that can operate effectively across a wide range of software applications, overcoming the limitations of training data sparsity for niche domains.

papers.title: Stabilizing Rubric Integration Training via Decoupled Advantage Normalization
papers.innovation: Proposes Process-Aware Policy Optimization (PAPO), a method that integrates process-level evaluation into Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) using decoupled advantage normalization. This addresses the limitations of outcome-reward models by incorporating intermediate process feedback for more stable and effective reinforcement learning.
papers.benchmarks: Aims to improve the stability and performance of reinforcement learning (RL) training by providing more comprehensive reward signals, leading to better policy optimization compared to methods relying solely on final-answer outcomes.
papers.use_case: Engineers working on reinforcement learning systems, especially in scenarios where evaluating the process leading to an outcome is as important as the outcome itself, enabling more robust policy learning and faster convergence in complex tasks.
